Now, you got to make sure you make that promise. So you got to know what the average dentist is making and kind of what the top end is making and can you create that kind of income replacement in 28 months or 36 months or 42 months with whatever the hell it is. The other thing to keep in mind in this scenario and in most others is, and a lot of opportunity marketers screw this up. They don't know this. We know this is something we know that they don't know, is you always want to do AB option because 20% of people will take the more expensive option. So somehow there's got to be, I don't know, a team of CPAs and lawyers and stuff that is accessible to them, sort of prepaid, prepaid legal financial title contract. So a back room, something that 20% of them can say yes to and give you a lot more money for because they will. Thank you so much. Okay. Let's go back, you were next, right? We'll get you all, but we're not going to get anybody else. If you had a client that was contemplating starting a religion slash lifestyle, what are some of the high level advice, insights that you would provide them as a starting point? Well, I mean, it doesn't change, right? So, other than the, so there's like, grandfatherly advice about it, that whether religion or personal development business, that you kind of have to be willing to be an appropriate figure for that religion. So, see if I'm a sales trainer or I'm doing this, if I wanted to, I could go into bar with you guys afterwards and I could have a few pops. And I used to, I just don't have any to drink now and I don't like to socialize. So, those two things, you know. And if I was single, I could maybe even hit on people and it would be okay, right? But if I'm doing personal growth, that all is sort of off the table. If I'm doing health, it's off the table. And if I'm a spiritual leader, it's off the table. Now that does not preclude people in those categories from doing it, but it usually does not end well. So, the grandfatherly advice is you've gotta be sure you wanna take on the role, right? But the business advice is the same. So, you first make a study of historical and contemporary. I mean, you know, why go to a room, right? I'm trying to figure out how they get the donate, but I don't, you know, it doesn't matter. But so, they're using every media. They're using online, they're using television, they're using radio, they're using print, they're using direct mail. Obviously, you wanna get on a direct mail list. So, you gotta now pick a bunch of them and make a donation. And really only need to do three or four if you're patient because of course they will take the list. So, you know, turn the TV on on Sunday, pick four or five of them and send money. You will now, your mailbox will be full. And then you make some judgments about commonalities. So, what are the essential elements here, right? That have to be here. So, see, like in the opportunity industry per se, there's certain essential elements, right? In a Yellow Pages ad, a phone number, a location, and if they're gonna come to your business, a map, are essential elements. Then there's optional things we decide to do, but we gotta know what the essential elements are, right? And so, and then I would, you know, build the profile of who it is I'm gonna direct my thing at, and then craft the thing. So, the fact that it is a secular religion or spiritual movement or whatever, doesn't matter much. And the big evangelical organizations, they're paying top dollar. So, they got the best copywriters, they got the best marketers, they got them. I mean, because they can, say look, they're running a business with no income taxes. So, immediately they got a 40% cost advantage over you and me. So, they're running a business with no income taxes. In most states, no property taxes for their facilities, a lot of volunteer labor, so the envelopes are being stuffed for free. See, they don't need a cat that licks stamps, they got volunteers who lick stamps. I mean, now, figuratively, not because they use a machine, but you get my point. So, their economic advantage is profound, right? And they have part of their promise, as a promise none of us can make, which is eternal salvation.
